title: SoFi Reports Record Revenue and Loan Origination
text: SoFi announced strong Q1 2026 earnings with net revenue of $1.1 billion, representing 43% year-over-year growth. The company also achieved record loan originations of $12.2 billion, up 68% from the prior year. The digital bank's total assets reached $53.7 billion, a 42% increase from the previous year. SoFi's adjusted revenue of $1.087 billion exceeded Wall Street consensus estimates by 3.6%, and the company reported net income of $166 million ($0.12 per share), matching expectations. Despite beating quarterly projections, SoFi maintained its 2026 revenue guidance at 30% growth. CEO Anthony Noto explained that the company went into the year expecting at least two rate cuts, a key assumption for revenue guidance, and cited the Federal Reserve's expected rate freeze and macroeconomic uncertainty as reasons for the conservative outlook. Stock shares fell 13% midday Wednesday, marking the largest single-day decline in a year. However, William Blair analysts maintained an outperform rating, noting they see SoFi steadily taking share from traditional banks. SoFi launched home equity lines of credit and Big Business Banking, an integrated platform enabling enterprise customers to manage fiat and cryptocurrency assets on a fully regulated basis.
